slides.title: The power rule
slides.body: Drag the exponent down. It multiplies out front and the power drops by one. Keep going to differentiate again.

slides.title: Match each function to its derivative
slides.body: Apply the power rule, then pair each function with the correct derivative.

feedback.wrong: Some matches are off. Remember: the exponent comes down and the power drops by one.

slides.title: The sum rule
slides.body: Move the point. On each curve the tangent arrow shows the slope. The slope of f + g is just the slope of f plus the slope of g.

slides.title: Differentiate the sum
slides.body: Use the power rule on each term, then add. Any equivalent form is accepted.

feedback.wrong: Differentiate each term separately and add: (x²)′ + (3x)′.

slides.title: The chain rule
slides.body: For a function inside a function, differentiate the outside, then multiply by the derivative of the inside.

slides.title: Differentiate the composite
slides.body: Apply the chain rule. You can leave it factored or expand it. Either form is accepted.

feedback.wrong: Outer first: 2(3x + 2). Then multiply by the inside's derivative, (3x + 2)′ = 3.

slides.title: The Mean Value Theorem
slides.body: Place the two endpoints to draw a secant. Somewhere between them, the tangent runs exactly parallel, the same slope as the secant.

slides.title: Apply the Mean Value Theorem
slides.body: Points A and B sit on the curve. Use the given equations to work through the two parts.

feedback.wrong: Recall that secant slope = (f(b) − f(a)) / (b − a)

slides.title: Combine the rules
slides.body: This one needs the chain rule, the power rule, and the sum rule together. Any equivalent form is accepted.

feedback.wrong: Differentiate each piece: chain rule on (2x + 1)², power rule on 3x³, then add.
